title: 30o: download_blob missing {type} substitution
description:
Location: crates/jmap-chat/src/blob.rs, download_blob function (line 128-174).

What is wrong: The download_blob function expands only three template variables: {accountId}, {blobId}, and {name}. It does not accept a content_type parameter and does not substitute {type}. RFC 8620 §6.2 states 'The URL MUST contain variables called accountId, blobId, type, and name' and defines {type} as 'The type for the server to set in the Content-Type header of the response'. The RFC example downloadUrl is 'https://jmap.example.com/download/{accountId}/{blobId}/{name}?accept={type}'.

Why it matters: Any production JMAP server following RFC 8620 will include {type} in its downloadUrl template. With the current implementation, {type} is left as the literal string '{type}' in the constructed URL. This will produce a malformed URL that the server will reject with 400 or 404, making download_blob non-functional against any compliant server. The tests use a template without {type} so they do not catch this.

Fix required: Add a content_type parameter (e.g. &str defaulting to 'application/octet-stream') to download_blob, include it in the expand_url_template call as ('type', content_type), and update all call sites and tests.
close_reason: duplicate of 30o.18 which was already fixed: accept_type param added to download_blob
